query: Frame the problem around our checkout abandonment rate. We've seen a 35% drop-off at the payment step over the last quarter. It affects all e-commerce customers on our platform. Our PM flagged it after a Mixpanel funnel analysis showed the decline started after we migrated payment providers in January.

context.preconditions: No prior artifacts exist for this feature. The project directory does not yet exist.

expected_behavior: Skill initiates interactive Q&A starting with a working title confirmation (e.g., 'Checkout Abandonment') before proceeding section by section
Skill drafts a Situation Context section covering the 35% drop-off, payment step, e-commerce customers, and the Mixpanel trigger, then asks for confirmation
Skill produces a Problem Statement in the '[Who] struggles with [what] because [why], which results in [consequence]' format and stress-tests it
Skill runs an adaptive 5 Whys root cause analysis, stopping when a genuine actionable root cause is reached rather than forcing 5 levels
Skill proposes 2-3 alternative framings (shifting who, what, or scope) and asks the user to choose before writing PROBLEM-FRAME.md to docs/features/checkout-abandonment/PROBLEM-FRAME.md

query: I feel like our onboarding is broken.

context.preconditions: User provides a vague, one-sentence description with no data, no affected users, no trigger event, and no working title.

expected_behavior: Skill asks a targeted follow-up question to gather missing context: who is affected, what makes the user think onboarding is broken, any data or feedback
Skill does not skip any section despite thin input — it asks specific questions to fill each gap rather than generating placeholder content
Skill redirects if the user provides solutions instead of problems (e.g., 'We need to redesign the wizard' triggers 'That sounds like a solution. What is the problem it solves?')
Skill derives a working title from the conversation and confirms it before proceeding (e.g., 'Onboarding Drop-off' rather than 'Add Onboarding Wizard')

query: We need to frame the problem — basically we need to add a caching layer because our API is too slow.

context.preconditions: User jumps directly to a solution (caching layer). No PROBLEM-FRAME.md exists. The skill should walk back from the solution to uncover the actual problem.

expected_behavior: Skill redirects the user from the proposed solution: 'A caching layer sounds like a solution. What is the problem it solves? What is slow or broken today?'
Skill walks back from the solution through structured Q&A to uncover the actual pain (e.g., API response times causing user drop-off)
Skill runs root cause analysis that may discover the root cause is unoptimized database queries rather than missing cache, opening the solution space
Skill frames the problem around the root cause (e.g., response time degradation) rather than the initially proposed solution (caching)
Skill writes PROBLEM-FRAME.md and logs the framing decision to DECISION.md in the same feature directory
